_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to review and edit our own texts and others' to improve clarity, coherence and control over content, organisation, sentence structure, vocabulary and punctuation.

_source.w: We are learning to review and edit our own texts and others' to improve clarity, coherence and control over content, organisation, sentence structure, vocabulary and punctuation.
y: Comprehensive review and editing at this level involves attention to every dimension of writing quality.
This systematic approach to revision is essential for producing polished, professional texts.
The ability to identify and improve specific aspects of writing demonstrates advanced literacy.
eg: We review a text systematically — checking content, then organisation, then sentence-level features.
We improve coherence by strengthening connections between paragraphs and ideas.
We refine sentence structures for clarity, variety and impact.
We select more precise vocabulary to replace vague or repeated words.
We proofread meticulously for punctuation and grammatical accuracy.
We provide detailed, constructive feedback on peers' writing that addresses multiple dimensions of quality.
